channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: IDF Extends Death Penalty Law to West Bank Palestinians Convicted of Terror
item.description: Israel Defense Forces Central Command chief has applied a new death penalty law to Palestinians in the West Bank convicted of terrorism, expanding capital punishment beyond Israel proper.

item.title: Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships to Reverse Navy Decision
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations says abandoning nuclear-powered surface combatants was one of the worst decisions the service ever made, as the new Trump class battleships will feature nuclear propulsion.

item.title: Argentina Retires A-4 Fightinghawks After Six Decades
item.description: Argentina has finally stood down its A-4 Skyhawks, the type's first export operator, after 60 years of service as the country introduces its first F-16s.

item.title: US Army Cancels Deployment of 4,000 Soldiers to Poland Abruptly
item.description: Lawmakers questioned the timing and reasons for the sudden cancellation, with Rep. Don Bacon saying it sends a "terrible message to Russia and our allies."

item.title: Norway Arrests Chinese Man for Spying
item.description: Norway's interior security service has arrested a Chinese national on suspicion of espionage, marking another case of alleged Chinese intelligence activity in Europe.

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization designated the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with at least 80 deaths reported and no approved vaccines for this strain.

item.title: Americans in Congo Potentially Exposed to Ebola in Latest Outbreak
item.description: Several Americans currently in Democratic Republic of Congo are believed to have had exposure to suspected Ebola cases, with some deemed high-risk exposures, as the outbreak spreads.

item.title: Cuba Accuses US of Fabricating Drone Threat Claims
item.description: Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez dismissed US intelligence reports about Cuban drone acquisitions from Russia and Iran as a "fraudulent case" designed to justify sanctions and potential military intervention.

item.title: Ukraine Drone Attack Kills Four in Moscow's Biggest Strike in Over a Year
item.description: At least four people were killed after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in more than a year, demonstrating Kyiv's ability to strike targets over 300 miles from the border despite dense Russian air defenses.

item.title: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Transdniestria Separatists
item.description: Moldovan leaders denounced President Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria region as a threat to sovereignty, amid escalating tensions over the separatist enclave.

item.title: Iran Excludes Star Forward Azmoun from Preliminary World Cup Squad
item.description: Iran's national team left out attacking talisman Sardar Azmoun from its preliminary World Cup squad as the team prepares for the tournament amid ongoing diplomatic tensions over participation.

item.title: Air Show Collision Under Investigation at Idaho Air Force Base
item.description: Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho is investigating an aircraft collision during an air show performance, with no immediate reports of casualties.

item.title: Bond Yield Spike Poses Risk to Unprepared Equities Market
item.description: Rising benchmark yields threaten equity valuations as companies and consumers face higher borrowing costs, with investors warning that markets may be underestimating the impact on growth and corporate profits.

item.title: Ukraine launches largest drone attack on Moscow, killing four
item.description: Ukraine sent nearly 600 drones into Russia overnight in the largest single attack on Moscow since the war began, killing at least four people and wounding a dozen more. The strike represents a significant escalation in Ukraine's deep-strike capabilities against the Russian capital.

item.title: Drone strike hits UAE nuclear power plant as Iran war deadlock persists
item.description: A drone strike caused a fire at the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, with Emirati officials investigating the source. The incident comes as US-Iran negotiations remain stalled and tensions remain high across the Gulf region.

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak spreading across Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, signaling the need for coordinated international response to contain the deadly virus.

item.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace negotiations stall
item.description: President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran that 'the clock is ticking' as ceasefire negotiations remain deadlocked. The US and Iran remain at impasse over ending the war, with neither side agreeing to terms for a permanent peace deal.

item.title: China agrees to buy at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually
item.description: The White House announced that China has committed to purchasing at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually following the Beijing summit between Trump and Xi Jinping. The agreement represents a significant trade concession from Beijing amid ongoing tariff tensions.

item.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts drones entering from Iraq amid regional tensions
item.description: Saudi Arabia announced it intercepted and destroyed three drones that entered its airspace from Iraq. The incident comes as Gulf states face escalating threats from the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Netanyahu faces potential political downfall as Israeli government teeters
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Binyamin Netanyahu may be facing his final days in power as political pressures mount. The Economist analyzes whether the long-serving leader can survive the current crisis.

item.title: Gulf states push to restore maritime navigation as Iran faces trust deficit
item.description: A Gulf official told Al Arabiya that Iran must work to restore trust with GCC states. Regional powers are stepping up efforts to secure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Separatist Region
item.description: Moldovan leaders denounced Russian President Vladimir Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria separatist enclave as a threat, contemplating measures to counteract it.

item.title: Israel to Build Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel cabinet approved building a defense compound on the site of recently demolished UNRWA premises in East Jerusalem. The UN had called the demolition a violation of international law.

item.title: Republicans Vie to Replace Defeated Senator Cassidy in Louisiana
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the primary after his impeachment vote against Trump. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming advance to the runoff.

item.title: Air Force Base Locked Down After Idaho Air Show Incident
item.description: Emergency crews responded to an aircraft incident during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho. Witnesses reported mid-air collision involving Navy jets with parachutes seen descending.
